summary: Excellent sound‑board capture of a high‑energy 10/20/84 Syracuse show highlighted by a monster Jack Straw, ripping Shakedown Street and an unforgettable second set.
review: The recording is a solid SBD that preserves the band’s raw power despite the Carrier Dome’s notorious reverb.  Fans rave about a blistering Jack Straw, a funk‑filled Shakedown Street and a soaring second‑set climax (Bird Song → The Other One → Black Peter).  Jerry’s guitar work shines, Brent’s keyboards add sparkle, and Phil’s bass drives the groove, even if a few vocal flubs and the dome’s acoustics temper perfection.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 50 reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (standout Jack Straw, Shakedown, Bird Song) and note a good‑to‑excellent soundboard source; only minor complaints about vocal strain or dome acoustics keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear guitar and keyboard mix, noticeable dome reverb and occasional vocal flubs; no drop‑outs.  Listeners note a pleasant delay on Garcia’s vocals and a generally balanced SBD despite the venue’s acoustics.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds from tight Bertha/West LA into a soaring Bird Song; second set ignites with Shakedown Street, then a monster Jack Straw that fuels an extended jam leading through The Other One and Black Peter before closing on a heartfelt Lovelight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for searing guitar tone on Jack Straw, wah‑rich solos on Shakedown, and emotional vocals (though some note vocal strain).
band_member_comments.Phil: Consistently lauded for driving bass lines (‘bomb zone’) that lock the rhythm section together.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for solid rhythm work and occasional piano/organ contributions; praised on Bird Song and Samson.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland highlighted for funky wah‑effects, jazz‑fusion piano on Shakedown, and tasteful delay vocals.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart noted for ferocious, steady beats that power the high‑energy second set.
